steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The passage gives two specific examples (Wisconsin 1983, Vermont 1985) and then the blank introduces a general conclusion: that multiple years often pass between selection and official designation. The relationship is generalization from examples — the examples support a broader, frequently-true claim.

steps.title: Match the relationship to the choices and eliminate
steps.content:
A. In many cases, — signals a frequent but not universal pattern; correctly introduces the generalization drawn from the examples. Correct.
B. Therefore, — signals a logical conclusion that must follow; the examples are illustrations, not logical premises that force the conclusion. Eliminated.
C. Indeed, — signals confirmation of something already stated; no such claim has been made yet. Eliminated.
D. Similarly, — signals a comparison between two parallel items; there is only one claim here, not two parallel ones. Eliminated.

A is correct.
